Scheduling Rounds

The method for scheduling rounds is specified in the XOnlineCompetitionCreateSingleElimination function's parameters. In particular, the pDefaultAttributes parameter is of type XONLINE_COMP_SINGLE_ELIMINATION_ATTRIBUTES. Use this structure's Interval and UnitOrMask members to set the interval between rounds. The interval can be a set number of minutes or hours. Rounds can also be held daily or weekly.

To specify the interval between rounds, you must store the interval type (minute, hour, daily, or weekly) in Interval. If the interval type is minutes, hours, or weeks, store the number of minutes, hours, or weeks in dwUnitsOfTime. For example, to specify that the competition's rounds occur every two hours, set Interval to XONLINE_COMP_INTERVAL_HOUR and dwUnitsOfTime.DayMask to 2.

Daily intervals are handled a bit differently. They use a day mask to indicate the days of the week by which rounds must be played. For example, to make a competition with rounds that occur every Monday, Wednesday, and Friday, set Interval to XONLINE_COMP_INTERVAL_DAILY and dwUnitsOfTime.DayMask to (XONLINE_COMP_DAY_MASK_MONDAY | XONLINE_COMP_DAY_MASK_WEDNESDAY | XONLINE_COMP_DAY_MASK_FRIDAY).

Note    All rounds are scheduled relative to the time specified in the ftRoundOneStart member of the XONLINE_COMP_SINGLE_ELIMINATION_ATTRIBUTES structure that is passed to XOnlineCompetitionCreateSingleElimination.
